verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a single-pass TM protein predominantly resident in the ER lumen but with a documented, functional plasma membrane pool. The protein has its own TM anchor and reaches the surface via regulated dual localization (ER-PM cycling), particularly upon calcium store depletion. PM STIM1 is stabilized by N-glycosylation and participates in store-operated calcium entry signaling. This meets the dual_localization criterion: the protein has constitutive PM residence (though minor) plus state-dependent PM enrichment.
